differential_diagnoses: Heavy metal poisoning (e.g., lead or mercury)
Inborn error of metabolism with neuromuscular involvement
next_steps: Send stool specimen for botulinum toxin and Clostridium botulinum culture
Consult neurology for nerve conduction studies and electromyography (repetitive nerve stimulation)
Consult infectious disease for consideration of empiric administration of Botulism Immune Globulin (BabyBIG)
Detailed history regarding dietary intake (honey, corn syrup) and environmental exposures
Serum analysis for heavy metal panel and ammonia/lactate/acylcarnitine profile
Continue supportive care and mechanical ventilation
Obtain stool for C. botulinum culture and toxin assay (California Department of Public Health)
Consult neurology for repetitive nerve stimulation study and EMG
Perform a thorough skin examination of the entire scalp and body looking for an attached tick
Initiate empiric BabyBIG (Botulism Immune Globulin) if clinical suspicion for botulism remains high
Measure serum electrolytes, including ionized calcium, magnesium, and phosphate
Maintain strict respiratory support and monitor for further bulbar compromise
Obtain detailed history regarding exposure to honey, corn syrup, soil, and dust
